title: VPN Tunnel Stability
spl:
index=vpn sourcetype="cisco:asa"
| where action IN ("session_connect","session_disconnect")
| transaction user maxspan=1h
| where eventcount > 4
| table user, eventcount, duration
| sort -eventcount
description: Frequent disconnects indicate network issues, client problems, or infrastructure instability affecting user productivity.
value: Network operations and helpdesk teams identify users experiencing VPN tunnel instability (frequent reconnections), distinguishing between endpoint issues, network problems, and concentrator-side failures to reduce ticket resolution time.
implementation: Track connect/disconnect patterns per user. Identify users with >4 reconnections per hour. Correlate with network quality metrics. Alert on widespread instability (multiple users affected simultaneously). Report for helpdesk.
visualization: Table (unstable connections), Bar chart (reconnects by user), Line chart (reconnection rate trend).
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic
  by All_Traffic.user, All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest span=1h
| sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Anomalies from new SaaS app rollouts, browser updates, or vendor URL category changes.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Splunk Add-on for Cisco ASA (`sourcetype=cisco:asa`). Data in `index=vpn`.
• Key fields: `action` (session_connect/session_disconnect), `user`, `src`, `duration` (session duration in seconds at disconnect).
• Tunnel instability manifests as rapid connect-disconnect cycles (flapping). This degrades user productivity and may indicate network issues, endpoint software conflicts, or VPN client bugs.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Verify connect/disconnect pairs:
```spl
index=vpn sourcetype="cisco:asa" earliest=-24h
| where action IN ("session_connect", "session_disconnect")
| stats count by action, user
| where count > 4
```

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Primary search — Tunnel flapping detection:
```spl
index=vpn sourcetype="cisco:asa" earliest=-4h
| where action IN ("session_connect", "session_disconnect")
| transaction user maxspan=4h
| where eventcount > 6
| eval reconnects=floor(eventcount/2)
| eval avg_session_sec=if(isnotnull(duration), round(duration/reconnects, 0), null())
| stats count as flap_episodes max(reconnects) as max_reconnects values(src) as source_ips by user
| where max_reconnects > 3
| sort - max_reconnects
```

Understanding this SPL: The `transaction` command groups connect/disconnect events per user. More than 6 events (3+ reconnect cycles) in 4 hours indicates instability. Very short average session durations (under 60 seconds) suggest the tunnel is dropping immediately after connecting — often a routing conflict, MTU issue, or endpoint software conflict. Multiple source IPs for the same user may indicate they are switching between networks (Wi-Fi to cellular).

Short session detection:
```spl
index=vpn sourcetype="cisco:asa" action="session_disconnect" earliest=-24h
| where duration < 60
| stats count as short_sessions dc(user) as affected_users by host
```

Step 3 — Validate
(a) Ask a user experiencing VPN instability to check their AnyConnect event log: it records disconnect reasons.
(b) On ASA: `show vpn-sessiondb | grep <user>` during an active session, then check again after a reported disconnect.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard: Row 1 — "Users experiencing flapping (4h)", "Short sessions (<60s)". Row 2 — Table: flapping users. Row 3 — Timechart: reconnects over 24h.
Alert: Trigger when any user reconnects >5 times in 1h.

Runbook:
1. **Widespread flapping across many users** — Network-side issue. Check the VPN concentrator for resource exhaustion, IP pool depletion, or certificate expiry.
2. **Single user flapping** — Endpoint issue. Check for conflicting VPN clients, firewall software, or unstable internet connection.
3. **Short sessions after client update** — AnyConnect update may have introduced a bug. Roll back or patch.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **`duration` field missing** — ASA may not include duration in disconnect events for all tunnel types. Calculate from connect-disconnect timestamps.
• **Transaction too large** — For very active users, `maxspan=4h` may capture too many events. Reduce to `maxspan=1h`.
grandmaExplanation: We detect when someone's secure connection keeps dropping and reconnecting, which disrupts their work, and we figure out whether the problem is on their end or ours.
hardware: Cisco ASA 5506-X/5508-X/5516-X/5525-X/5545-X/5555-X, ASAv, Cisco Secure Firewall 3100/4200; Palo Alto PA-220/PA-440/PA-3200/PA-5200, GlobalProtect; Fortinet FortiGate 60F/100F/200F/600F/1800F, FortiGate SSL-VPN; Juniper SRX300/SRX1500/SRX4100/SRX4200, Junos Dynamic VPN
